edit.readOnly: Read-only mode: you don't have permissions to edit venue information.
edit.notFoundDesc: The venue you are looking for does not exist or you don't have access to it.

descriptions.operationalRole: Identifies a store, distribution center, or hybrid operation without changing its fiscal business type.
descriptions.salesEnabled: Allows orders and charges to be created at this venue.
descriptions.cedisSalesDisabled: A distribution-only venue cannot record sales; the backend also blocks older clients.

subtitles.basicInfo: Name, business type and general venue details
subtitles.contact: Public contact details, logo and cover photo for your venue

documents.documentRejected: This document was rejected. Please resubmit it.
documents.comprobanteDomicilioDesc: Utility bill, water, or phone receipt

google.title: Google Business Profile
google.description: Connect your Google Business Profile to sync reviews and respond directly from the dashboard
google.notConnectedDesc: Connect your Google Business Profile to:
google.benefit1: Automatically sync Google reviews to your dashboard
google.benefit2: Respond to Google reviews from one central location
google.benefit3: Use AI-powered response suggestions for faster engagement

mcp.title: Connect your AI to Avoqado
mcp.description: Use Claude, ChatGPT or another AI to ask your business — or request changes — in plain language.
mcp.intro: Connect your favorite AI to Avoqado and ask it "how are sales?", "what reservations do I have?", or tell it "cancel the 8pm one" — in plain language, only across your venues and within your role.
mcp.claudeIntro: From the Claude app or web (Pro plan or higher):
mcp.claudeStep2: Give it a name (e.g. "Avoqado") and paste this URL:
mcp.claudeStep3: Sign in with your Avoqado email and password. Done!
mcp.cliNote: Prefer the terminal? With Claude Code:
mcp.claudePromptNote: 💬 …or paste this prompt into Claude Code and it sets itself up:
mcp.claudePrompt: Add the Avoqado MCP and connect me: run "claude mcp add --transport http avoqado {{url}}" then guide me to sign in.
mcp.claudeWebNote: Note: in Claude web or desktop the connection is added under Connectors (the steps above). For security it can't connect itself from a prompt — that only works in Claude Code in the terminal.
mcp.codexStep2: Then sign in (opens the browser to authorize):
mcp.codexPromptNote: 💬 …or paste this prompt into Codex and it sets itself up:
mcp.codexPrompt: Add the Avoqado MCP and connect me: run "codex mcp add avoqado --url {{url}}" then "codex mcp login avoqado".
mcp.note: Safe by design: you only see and change what your role allows, scoped to your venues. Nothing crosses into other businesses.

dangerZone.description: Actions in this section are irreversible. Proceed with caution.
